name: CONSTRAINT2_c
description: Dual output constraint block for continuous systems - enforces constraints with two outputs

exprs.description: Complex exprs structure with nested ScilabString arrays

xmlExample:
<BasicBlock id="b1" parent="0:2:0" interfaceFunctionName="CONSTRAINT2_c" blockType="c" dependsOnU="0" dependsOnT="1" simulationFunctionName="constraint_c" simulationFunctionType="IMPLICIT_C_OR_FORTRAN" style="CONSTRAINT2_c">
  <Array as="exprs" scilabClass="ScilabList">
    <ScilabString height="1" width="1">
      <data line="0" column="0" value="0"/>
    </ScilabString>
    <ScilabString height="1" width="1">
      <data line="0" column="0" value="0"/>
    </ScilabString>
    <ScilabString height="1" width="1">
      <data line="0" column="0" value="0"/>
    </ScilabString>
  </Array>
  <ScilabInteger as="integerParameters" height="1" width="1" intPrecision="sci_int32">
    <data line="0" column="0" value="0"/>
  </ScilabInteger>
  <ScilabDouble as="state" height="1" width="2">
    <data line="0" column="0" realPart="0.0"/>
    <data line="0" column="1" realPart="0.0"/>
  </ScilabDouble>
  <mxGeometry as="geometry" x="0.0" y="-10.0" width="60.0" height="40.0"/>
</BasicBlock>
anomalies: ANOMALY: exprs is ScilabList containing 3 nested ScilabString elements (unusual structure)
No realParameters - uses integerParameters and state only
Same simulationFunctionName as CONSTRAINT_c but with different output configuration
criticalRules: exprs is an Array typed as ScilabList with 3 ScilabString children
Used when constraint error and correction signals are needed separately
commonUses: DAE systems requiring dual constraint outputs
